lineage_note: Design/manifest round. This lineage is the ORCHESTRATOR: it seeds the fresh workspace + premise, spawns blind subagents, and re-derives every pin on a fresh rebuild. It does NOT author, repair, render, or judge the narrative (R469 contamination bound). The deterministic negative-control probes (PIN-2) are VERIFICATION, not narrative authoring — the R570 remove-and-rescan pattern. Self-report is not trusted (R500): pins are verified from the agent's JSON artifacts, never its store or log.
question: The R600 proof gap: the continuity gate's RULE CLASSES (exclusive/interval/transition = possession-custody / numeric-timing / state-machine) were NEVER exercised by the unattended loop — v1/v2's games contained no rule-tripping content. A grounded pre-check sharpens WHY: describe-schema documents the rule CLASSES + parameters, but NOT the rules-FILE wire format nor the `[continuity].rules_path` wiring (the canon-order file has `[continuity].canon_order_path` documented; the rules file has no equivalent), so the loop was never told how to TURN THE RULES ON. Does a blind loop agent, given a premise whose world-logic REQUIRES all three classes and a brief to enforce that logic with the gate, (a) DECLARE all three rule classes from the surface, (b) TYPE the content so the gate keys on it, (c) does the gate BITE on a real rule violation (deterministic negative control on the agent's own store), and (d) does the loop self-repair? = closes R600's 'rule classes untested' gap, OR names the surface (the rules-file authoring wire) that blocks it.
premise: premise.md — 'The Night Lock at Harrow Sluice', a fresh IP-free small game: a lock-keeper's apprentice works a canal lock alone through a storm night; a late barge signals in the dark. Required shape carries THREE hard world-invariants that force the three rule classes: (1) ONE storm-lantern passed hand-to-hand = custody (exclusive per:object); (2) the lock-gate state machine barred->cracked->open->cracked->barred, never a barred->open jump = state-machine (transition); (3) the upper pound must FILL for >= a set number of minutes before the gate may open = numeric timing (interval). Plus the v1/v2 shape: 14-20 scenes, 4-6 frames, a two-branch fork (work the lock for the barge, or hold it till dawn), a withheld secret (disclosure plan), one quest, setup->payoff chains. The agent authors all facts + the rules file; the orchestrator only seeds the premise.

firewall.rule: R469 contamination bound. The blind loop agent (Step 1) authors the store + the narrative rules file + self-repairs, with NO orchestrator help and NO hint about the rules-file wire format or wiring (that is the surface under test — it must come from describe-schema or the agent's own error-reading). A separate blind render agent + judge handle the coherence read. This lineage runs only the deterministic setup + verification + gates + the PIN-2 negative-control probes.
blind_subagents: loop agent (author facts + rules + gate + repair, unattended)
orchestrator_may: seed the premise + fresh empty workspace
run describe-schema / propose-verdict / import-* / report-* / validate-workspace for VERIFICATION
author the PIN-2 negative-control probe manifests (minimal rule-violating facts) and run them through propose-verdict = the R570 remove-and-rescan teeth
orchestrator_may_not: author or repair the agent's store, facts, or rules file
hint the loop agent about the rules-file wire / wiring / the three rules to declare beyond the premise's stated world-invariants

deterministic_pins.PIN-1_declared_and_typed: On a FRESH rebuild from the agent's sections.json + facts*.json + its rules file + its canon-order artifact + its mnemosyne.toml wiring, the loaded rules contain >= 1 rule of EACH class (exclusive, transition, interval), each keying on a predicate that load-bearing typed legs in facts actually use, and validate-workspace + propose-verdict (idempotent re-apply = commit) + report-authoring-frontier --telling <plan> (0 zero-fact / 0 unordered / 0 dangling / 0 unresolved) are clean. FALSIFIABLE + informative: a class the agent could NOT declare from the surface is ABSENT here — PIN-1 records exactly which classes landed, and the loop-log names the surface gap for any that did not (the deliverable). never-planned disclosures may be > 0.
deterministic_pins.PIN-2_teeth_bite: For EACH rule the agent declared, the orchestrator applies ONE minimal rule-VIOLATING mutation to a throwaway clone of the agent's final store (via a probe facts manifest through propose-verdict, with the agent's rules wired) and the continuity gate REJECTS with THAT rule's violation, AND the same store WITHOUT the mutation passes (commit). exclusive: a second concurrent holder of the custody object in one (frame x world). transition: a successor state that is not a declared (from, to). interval: numeric legs whose value(left) - value(right) violates the op/bound. This proves the classes are LOAD-BEARING on the agent's real authored content (the R600 gap's core: the gate actually bites), and the paired PASS on removal proves the finding is the rule, not noise.
deterministic_pins.PIN-3_fired_in_loop_reported: From loop-log.md, RECORD (not a pass/fail gate on the agent's competence, but a required honest datum): whether the gate fired on a rule-class reject during the agent's OWN iterations (natural teeth) or the agent authored rule-consistent content first-try (then PIN-2's orchestrator injection is the only teeth evidence, and that is stated plainly, not dressed up). Also record every friction the agent hit declaring the rules (probe count, what it had to reverse-engineer) = the surface-sufficiency deliverable.

judged_no_pin: A blind render agent warm-renders one road (~1-2k words); a blind judge reads it and rates real-game (yes/no) + world-logic legibility (is the custody / gate-sequence / fill-timing felt on the page, not just typed?) + coherence 1-5. Guards store-consistency != coherence at the rule-stress level (a rule-passing husk would score low). Recorded, not gated — the experiment's PINS are the rule classes; the judge is the coherence backstop.

decision_rule_pre_committed.classes_exercised: PIN-1 (all 3 declared + typed) AND PIN-2 (all 3 bite + the paired removal passes) hold => the R600 proof gap is CLOSED: the rule classes are REACHABLE in the unattended loop and the gate BITES on real authored content. Deliverable = PIN-3's record of whether the loop declared them from the surface alone or hit a friction (and whether the gate fired naturally or only under the orchestrator's negative control).
decision_rule_pre_committed.surface_gap: PIN-2 holds for the classes that WERE declared, but PIN-1 is PARTIAL because the agent could not declare >= 1 class from the surface (the rules-file wire / `[continuity].rules_path` wiring is undocumented in describe-schema) => the honest finding = the rules-file AUTHORING SURFACE is missing from describe-schema (a build target, the R595 wire-format / R596 canon-order pattern); the classes that DID land still had their teeth proven. This is the pre-registered most-likely outcome (PRED-1).
decision_rule_pre_committed.teeth_dull: A rule the agent declared whose deterministic negative-control violation does NOT trigger the gate => a real gate BUG (highest priority, a build target the same session, the CLAUDE.md build-error-first ethos). The alarming outcome; PIN-2 is designed to catch it.
decision_rule_pre_committed.husk: PIN-1 + PIN-2 hold but the judged coherence read fails (not a real game / world-logic illegible) => store-consistency != causal-coherence reproduced at the rule-stress level: the loop produced a rule-passing husk. A measured residue, not a build.
decision_rule_pre_committed.contaminated: The loop-log shows the agent read crate source or was hinted the rules-file wire => inconclusive on surface-sufficiency; re-run with a tighter brief. Not a substrate finding.

grading_procedure: Orchestrator runs runbook.md Steps 0-5. PIN-1 verified on a fresh rebuild; PIN-2's negative-control probes authored + run by the orchestrator through propose-verdict; PIN-3 read from the loop-log. The render + judge run last, blind, one road. All pin outputs pasted verbatim into report.md.
predictions_preregistered: PRED-1 (GROUNDED, verified at design time): describe-schema documents the rule CLASSES + parameters but NOT the rules-FILE wire format nor the `[continuity].rules_path` wiring => the loop hits a friction declaring the rules (analogous to v1's 11 wire-format probes) => `surface_gap` is the most likely outcome, with the teeth proven (PIN-2) on whatever the agent managed to declare, possibly by reverse-engineering the load errors.

PRED-2: the exclusive(per:object) custody rule is the EASIEST to type (the R547/R549 idiom the loop reached for unprompted); transition needs the supersedes_in_frame succession chain (harder); interval needs numeric scalar predicates + a same-subject relation (least idiomatic) => interval is the class most likely to be absent or the most frictionful.

PRED-3: the gate will NOT fire naturally during the agent's own authoring (a competent agent authors rule-consistent content first-try), so PIN-2's deterministic negative control is the teeth evidence, not the loop-log — and R600's 'never exercised' is then closed by the orchestrator's injection, stated plainly (the honest limit, not dressed as the loop tripping its own gate).
honest_caveats: n = 1 premise / 1 loop agent / 1 render / 1 judge / one render road.

The PIN-2 negative control is an ORCHESTRATOR injection into the agent's store, not the agent's own error — it proves the gate BITES on the agent's real content, but NOT that the loop NATURALLY trips it (PRED-3 makes this explicit). Closing R600's 'the classes are untested' is exactly this: the teeth are proven load-bearing on authored content, whether or not the competent agent happened to trip them.

'unattended' means no human and no orchestrator help, not a weak agent — the claim is about the SURFACE's sufficiency (can a competent agent declare the rules from describe-schema?) and the GATE's teeth, not about a floor.
The premise is engineered to be rule-dense; a real game need not carry all three classes at once. The experiment tests reachability + teeth, not that rules are common.
